description: Proven fix approaches for Android adaptive UI patterns. Agent reads this at session start to skip re-reasoning on known patterns. Updated by scripts/sync_playbook.py.

patterns.problem: Deprecated calculateWindowSizeClass() API or enum equality checks that break when Large/ExtraLarge window classes are added.
approach: 1. Replace calculateWindowSizeClass(activity) with val adaptiveInfo = currentWindowAdaptiveInfo()
2. Replace widthSizeClass == WindowWidthSizeClass.Expanded with isWidthAtLeastBreakpoint(WIDTH_DP_EXPANDED_LOWER_BOUND)
3. Call currentWindowAdaptiveInfo() ONCE at composition root — pass windowSizeClass down as a plain parameter
4. Add @file:OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3AdaptiveApi::class) to every file using adaptive APIs
patterns.code_sketch:
val adaptiveInfo = currentWindowAdaptiveInfo()
val windowSizeClass = adaptiveInfo.windowSizeClass
val isExpanded = windowSizeClass.isWidthAtLeastBreakpoint(WIDTH_DP_EXPANDED_LOWER_BOUND)
constraints: currentWindowAdaptiveInfo() is @Composable — must be called in composable scope only
Never re-call deep in the tree — single call at root, pass result down

patterns.problem: BottomNavigation always renders as bottom bar regardless of screen size — tablets and foldables need a rail or permanent drawer.
approach: 1. Replace BottomNavigation { } with NavigationSuiteScaffold as the root composable
2. Replace BottomNavigationItem with item() inside navigationSuiteItems = { }
3. Remove the bottomBar = {} slot from Scaffold — NavigationSuiteScaffold IS the scaffold
4. NavigationSuiteScaffoldDefaults.calculateFromAdaptiveInfo(adaptiveInfo) handles Compact→Rail→Drawer automatically
5. Override layoutType only when the product requires a non-default mapping
constraints: NavigationSuiteScaffold replaces Scaffold as root — do not nest inside Scaffold's bottomBar slot
Requires material3-adaptive-navigation-suite Gradle dependency

patterns.problem: Column with 5+ children clips on compact-height screens such as phones in landscape.
approach: 1. Known fixed list: add Modifier.verticalScroll(rememberScrollState()) to the Column
2. Dynamic/large list: replace Column with LazyColumn — move children to items { } block
3. Infinite scroll with LazyColumn: see pattern 'infinite-scroll-lazycolumn'
constraints: Never combine .verticalScroll with LazyColumn — lazy lists manage their own scrolling
rememberScrollState() must be called inside a composable scope

patterns.problem: Infinite scroll load-more trigger fires on every scroll frame (causes excess recomposition) or is missing entirely (pagination never fires).
approach: 1. val listState = rememberLazyListState() — pass to LazyColumn(state = listState)
2. Derive trigger with derivedStateOf to avoid recomposition on every frame
3. val shouldLoadMore by remember { derivedStateOf { listState.layoutInfo.visibleItemsInfo.lastOrNull()?.index ?: 0 >= totalItems - LOAD_THRESHOLD } }
4. LaunchedEffect(shouldLoadMore) { if (shouldLoadMore && !isLoading) onLoadMore() }
5. Key each item with stable ID: items(items = list, key = { it.id }) { ... }
6. LOAD_THRESHOLD = 3..5 items — tune to network latency
constraints: derivedStateOf is the critical piece — without it, shouldLoadMore recomputes on every pixel of scroll
LaunchedEffect key = shouldLoadMore (not Unit) so it only fires when the boolean flips to true
Never use firstVisibleItemIndex alone — layoutInfo.visibleItemsInfo is the correct API for last-visible

patterns.problem: WindowInfoTracker collected without lifecycle binding leaks on config change, or posture not observed reactively at all.
approach: 1. Use rememberDevicePosture() from PostureDetector.kt — wraps WindowInfoTracker lifecycle safely
2. If rolling your own: WindowInfoTracker.getOrCreate(context).windowLayoutInfo.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
3. Map FoldingFeature to DevicePosture sealed class: NormalPosture, TableTopPosture, BookPosture, SeparatingPosture
4. Branch layout on posture — TableTop: top/bottom split; Book: left/right split
5. Size hinge Spacer from FoldingFeature.bounds — never hardcode hinge width
constraints: collectAsStateWithLifecycle requires lifecycle-runtime-compose dep
Never collect in a raw coroutine scope without lifecycle — leaks on rotation
SeparatingPosture (STATE_FLAT + isSeparating=true) is dual-screen, not a half-folded device

patterns.problem: Wear OS code imports androidx.compose.material3 (mobile MaterialTheme) — incompatible with wear.compose.material3, causes runtime crash or invisible UI.
approach: 1. Move all Wear composables to a separate :wear Gradle module (com.android.application)
2. :wear uses ONLY wear.compose.material3 — never androidx.compose.material3
3. :app (mobile) must not import androidx.wear.compose.* — add a lint check
4. Shared data models go in :common module if needed
5. Add wearApp project(':wear') to :app build.gradle.kts
constraints: The two MaterialTheme implementations share no common base — mixing them crashes at runtime
TransformingLazyColumn replaces ScalingLazyColumn — use the newer API

patterns.problem: setContent{} called inside a Car App Screen subclass — Auto does not support Compose rendering inside Screen.
approach: 1. Remove setContent{} from Screen subclasses entirely
2. Override onGetTemplate(): Template and return a Template (ListTemplate, MessageTemplate, etc.)
3. Navigate via screenManager.push(NextScreen(carContext))
4. Driver distraction: max 6 items on minCarApiLevel 1-2, use ItemList.Builder
5. Add <meta-data android:name='androidx.car.app.minCarApiLevel' android:value='1'/> inside <service> in manifest
constraints: No Compose inside Screen — template model only, no exceptions
6 item limit on minCarApiLevel 1-2 is enforced by the car host at runtime
app-projected and app-automotive are separate Gradle artifacts — never both in same module

patterns.problem: Text() without maxLines/overflow clips silently on compact screens or wraps without bound on large ones.
approach: 1. Titles / labels (single-line): maxLines = 1, overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis
2. Body text: maxLines = 3 (or appropriate), overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis, softWrap = true (default)
3. Error/status messages: maxLines = 2, overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis
4. overflow only activates when text exceeds maxLines — always set both together
constraints: TextOverflow.Clip is the default and silently truncates with no visual indicator — prefer Ellipsis
Setting overflow without maxLines has no effect — set both

patterns.problem: Master-detail layout shows only single pane on tablets/foldables — list and detail should render side by side on expanded widths.
approach: 1. val navigator = rememberListDetailPaneScaffoldNavigator<ItemType>()
2. Wrap each pane in AnimatedPane { } — required for predictive back gesture
3. List pane click: navigator.navigateTo(ListDetailPaneScaffoldRole.Detail, selectedItem)
4. Detail pane reads: navigator.currentDestination?.contentKey
5. BackHandler(navigator.canNavigateBack()) { navigator.navigateBack() }
6. @file:OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3AdaptiveApi::class) at file top
constraints: AnimatedPane is mandatory for predictive back gesture — omitting breaks the transition animation
Navigator generic type must match the type passed to navigateTo()
